critical.mandate: Every change request must have a formal impact analysis before any artifact updates begin
User must APPROVE the change after reviewing impact analysis — this is a hard gate
Cascade updates to downstream artifacts via subagents after approval

step.title: Capture Change Request
step.ask: Describe the change request: What needs to change and why?
What is driving this change? (user feedback / stakeholder request / technical discovery / market shift / regulatory)
Urgency level? (critical — blocks current sprint / high — next sprint / normal — backlog)
step.action: Assign CR ID: CR-{date}-{seq} (e.g., CR-2026-03-09-1). Check {planning_artifacts}/ for existing CR files to determine next sequence number.

step.title: Load All Artifacts
step.action: Read {planning_artifacts}/prd.md — extract requirement IDs (FR-*, NFR-*), feature section headers, user personas, NFR targets
Read {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — extract component list, API endpoints, data models, ADR references, tech stack
Read {planning_artifacts}/epics-and-stories.md — extract all story IDs, statuses, dependencies, sizes, risk levels
Read {implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml — current sprint ID, story statuses, velocity used vs available
Read {test_artifacts}/test-plan.md if exists — extract risk areas and high-risk components
Read {test_artifacts}/traceability-matrix.md if exists — extract requirement-to-test coverage mappings
Note any missing artifacts as INFO — they limit analysis depth but do not block the workflow

step.title: Requirements Impact Analysis
step.action: Analyze the change against PRD content:
— Identify PRD sections affected (features, personas, user journeys, NFRs, constraints)
— List requirement IDs (FR-*, NFR-*) that need modification, addition, or removal
— Assess NFR impact: does this change performance, security, scalability, or availability targets?
— Identify user journeys affected — which personas and flows change?
Classify requirements_impact: NONE (no PRD changes) / MINOR (wording, clarification) / SIGNIFICANT (new requirements, modified scope) / BREAKING (removes or contradicts existing requirements)

step.title: Technical Impact Analysis
step.action: Analyze the change against architecture:
— Identify architecture components affected (services, modules, layers)
— Assess API changes: new endpoints, modified contracts, breaking changes, versioning impact
— Assess data model changes: schema migrations needed, new entities, modified relationships
— Identify infrastructure impact: new services, scaling changes, deployment topology
— Check ADR implications: existing decisions invalidated? New architectural decisions needed?
Classify technical_impact: NONE / MINOR (config, minor refactor) / SIGNIFICANT (new component, API change) / BREAKING (architectural shift, tech stack change)

step.title: Sprint and Effort Impact Analysis
step.action: Analyze the change against current sprint and stories:
— Identify existing stories directly affected (scope changes, new blockers, invalidated acceptance criteria)
— If stories in 'in-progress' or 'review' are affected: flag as HIGH_RISK — these may need rework
— Estimate new stories needed: count and size (S=1pt, M=3pt, L=5pt, XL=8pt)
— Calculate total effort: sum of new story points + rework estimate for affected existing stories
— Assess sprint absorption: compare effort against remaining sprint velocity capacity
Classify sprint_impact: ABSORBABLE (fits in current sprint) / NEXT_SPRINT (defer to next sprint) / MULTI_SPRINT (requires multiple sprints)
If traceability-matrix.md exists: flag test coverage gaps that this change will create

step.title: Impact Summary and Approval Gate
step.ask: Review the impact analysis above. Do you APPROVE or REJECT this change request?
step.check: Set cr_status = REJECTED. Skip steps 7-9 — proceed directly to step 10 to generate the CR record.
Set cr_status = APPROVED. Proceed with artifact cascade updates.

step.title: Update PRD
step.action: Skip if requirements_impact = NONE.

Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_kratos/lifecycle/workflows/2-planning/edit-prd/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY. Context: This is change request {cr_id}. Apply these changes to the PRD: {change_description}. Use CR ID {cr_id} when prompted."
When subagent returns: verify prd.md version note references {cr_id}. Record: prd_updated = true.

step.title: Update Architecture
step.action: Skip if technical_impact = NONE.
If technical_impact = MINOR: flag for manual architecture review — "Run /kratos-adversarial targeting architecture.md after making manual updates."

If technical_impact = SIGNIFICANT or BREAKING: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY." When subagent returns: verify adversarial-review-{date}.md exists.
Record: architecture_reviewed = true, manual_update_needed = {true if SIGNIFICANT/BREAKING}.
If manual_update_needed: inform user — "Architecture needs manual updates for this change. Run /kratos-create-arch to revise, then re-run adversarial review."

step.title: Update Stories
step.condition: new_stories_needed OR existing_stories_affected AND cr_status == APPROVED
step.action: Skip if no new stories needed AND no existing stories affected.

If new stories needed: spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_kratos/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_kratos/lifecycle/workflows/4-implementation/add-stories/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY. Context: Adding stories for change request {cr_id}. Requirements: {new_story_descriptions}."
When subagent returns: verify epics-and-stories.md has new stories with Source: {cr_id}. Record: stories_added = {count}.
If existing stories need scope changes: list affected stories with what needs to change — flag for manual update via /kratos-fix-story.
If sprint injection needed: inform user — "Run /kratos-correct-course with CR ID {cr_id} to inject new stories into current sprint."

step.title: Generate Change Request Record
step.template-output: Generate change request document with YAML frontmatter: --- cr_id: "{cr_id}" date: "{date}" status: "{APPROVED|REJECTED}" urgency: "{critical|high|normal}" driver: "{source}" requirements_impact: "{NONE|MINOR|SIGNIFICANT|BREAKING}" technical_impact: "{NONE|MINOR|SIGNIFICANT|BREAKING}" sprint_impact: "{ABSORBABLE|NEXT_SPRINT|MULTI_SPRINT}" effort_estimate_points: {number} --- Then detailed sections: Change Description, Impact Analysis (requirements, technical, sprint), Affected Artifacts (what changed, what still needs manual update), Stories Created/Modified, Cascade Status (completed updates vs pending), and Recommended Next Actions. If REJECTED: include rejection reason and impact analysis for future reference.
step.action: If cr_status == APPROVED and stories were added: recommend "/kratos-correct-course with CR ID {cr_id}" or "/kratos-create-story {story_key}" as next steps.
If cr_status == APPROVED and manual architecture update needed: recommend "/kratos-create-arch to revise architecture".
